description: Build the Transactions page with full expense/income management - add, edit, delete, categorize, search, filter, and recurring transaction support. Also refactor the existing dashboard page to use the new storage layer and show a comprehensive financial overview.
steps: Create app/dashboard/transactions/page.tsx - Full transaction management page with: (1) Header with title + 'Add Transaction' button, (2) Filter bar with search input, category dropdown, type filter (all/expense/income), date range picker (this month, last month, last 3 months, custom), (3) Transaction list showing all transactions sorted by date (newest first) with description, category, amount (red for expense, green for income), date, and recurring badge if applicable, (4) Each transaction row has edit/delete action buttons, (5) Add/Edit modal/form with fields: type (expense/income), description, amount, category (dropdown with common categories + custom option), date, isRecurring toggle, recurringFrequency (daily/weekly/biweekly/monthly/yearly) if recurring, notes. (6) Pagination or 'load more' for large lists. Use the storage.ts service for all data operations.

Refactor app/dashboard/page.tsx - Update to use the new storage layer from app/lib/storage.ts. The dashboard overview should show: (1) Total income this month, (2) Total expenses this month, (3) Net balance (income - expenses), (4) Budget status summary (how many budgets are on track vs over), (5) Upcoming bills (next 7 days), (6) Savings progress (aggregate of all goals), (7) Recent transactions (last 5), (8) Spending by category chart (horizontal bars). Keep the personalized greeting and onboarding-based customization. Quick Actions should link to the actual pages now (Add Expense -> /dashboard/transactions, Set Budget -> /dashboard/budgets, View Reports -> /dashboard/reports).

Seed realistic mock data on first load - When no data exists in localStorage, generate a realistic set of mock transactions spanning the current month (15-20 transactions mixing expenses and income) that align with the user's onboarding categories and income range. This gives the app a populated feel on first use.
acceptance_criteria: Transactions page renders with search, filter, and transaction list
Users can add new transactions (both expense and income types)
Users can edit existing transactions inline or via modal
Users can delete transactions with confirmation
Recurring transactions are visually indicated with a badge
Dashboard page shows comprehensive financial overview with real data from storage layer
Quick Actions on dashboard link to actual functional pages
Mock data seeds on first load for a good initial experience
npm run build completes without errors
findings: React 19 strict lint rule (react-hooks/set-state-in-effect) prohibits calling setState inside useEffect. Fixed by using useState with lazy initializer function instead. The NotImplementedError warnings from bun worker_threads are harmless and do not affect the build.
